pairs.b_topic: Time Block Architecture for Dual-Track Growth
pairs.a_text: Alex Hormozi states that a 9-to-5 job provides two usable four-hour windows per day — 5 a.m. to 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. to 9 p.m. — and that wasting those windows on doom-scrolling, rather than skill-building or business activity, is the primary reason people fail to escape the income trap, not the job itself.
pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi claims that the 5am-9am and 5pm-9pm time windows (approximately 8 hours per day outside a day job) are the primary compounding hours for an early-career person who must simultaneously pay for today and build toward tomorrow.

pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi argues that the supplement industry ($123B globally) is roughly twice the size of the health club industry ($62B) despite both targeting the same goals (health, weight loss, energy), primarily because supplements have lower perceived effort and time delay, illustrating that the delivery vehicle for the same dream outcome determines price and volume, not the outcome itself.
